body.title: NE: defensive JSON parser — don't skip cycle on bad LLM response
body.result: _clean_llm_response strips fences/<think>; _call_inference retries once then returns fallback result; WARNING log on failure; 10 tests
body.description:
When the NE calls the upstream LLM and gets a malformed/non-JSON response, _call_inference() returns None and the NE skips the cycle silently (narrative_engine.py:1089-1091). Root cause confirmed in T-igor-upstream-reply-parse investigation.

The failure path:
  1. _gw().call('ne', prompt, ctx) returns non-JSON text
  2. _parse_ne_json() at line 1082 returns None
  3. Cycle logs NE_FAIL anomaly but skips with no recovery
  4. Igor has no goal output for this cycle and loops

Fix: (1) Add a retry with stripped markdown/think-block cleanup on first JSON parse fail. (2) If still None after cleanup, emit a minimal fallback NE result (impulse to re-examine TWM) rather than returning None. (3) Log the raw response (first 500 chars) at WARNING level so the log-error audit step can surface it.

**Affected files:** devices/igor/cognition/narrative_engine.py (_parse_ne_json, _call_inference)
**Design rules:** none — cognition is MEDIUM inertia; no HIGH-inertia files touched
**Scope boundary:** fix the parse-fail path in _call_inference() only. Do not change the prompt format or model routing.
**Test plan:** unit test: feed _call_inference a mock gateway that returns markdown-wrapped JSON; verify result is parsed not skipped. Feed one that returns pure garbage; verify fallback minimal result is returned.
